titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jim Fouratt Papers
titlestmt.author: by Jim Fisher and Michael Rush

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday July 27, 2019 at 03:47

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection spans the years 1965 to 2016 and consists of correspondence, writings, printed material, financial papers, photographs, audiocassettes, videocassettes, buttons, t-shirts, and other papers and realia relating to Jim Fouratt's life and involvement with political and counterculture activities, gay rights and AIDS activism, and nightclubs and the music industry. Papers relating to political and counterculture activism document Fouratt's involvement with movements and events of the late 1960s and early 1970s, including the Youth International Party (Yippies) and the 1967 Central Park Be In. Papers relating to gay rights and AIDS document Fouratt's activism and work with organizations including the Gay Liberation Front and ACT UP. Papers relating to nightclub management document his work at Hurrah!, Danceteria, and other clubs, and papers relating to band management document Fouratt's work with a wide range of musical acts. Band demos and video recordings document new wave, rock, and punk acts that performed or sought to perform at clubs managed by Fouratt during the early to mid-1980s. Buttons collected by Jim Fouratt span a variety of political causes and many musical acts active during the 1980s. T-shirts collected by Fouratt represent a range of musical acts, clubs, and political causes.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2015.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jim Fouratt Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Jim Fouratt
bioghist.p: Jim Fouratt (b. 1945) is an American gay rights activist, writer, nightclub promoter, and music industry manager. In the late 1960s and early 1970s Fouratt was a member of the Yippies and helped organize the Central Park Be In. He was present at the Stonewall Rebellion, co-founded the Gay Liberation Front, and has been an active contributor to many groups focused on gay rights and AIDS-related activism. Fouratt has worked in the music industry in several capacities, ranging from managing nightclubs such as Hurrah! and Danceteria in the late 1970s and early 1980s to working as vice president of A&R at Mercury Records in the late 1990s. He has also worked as an actor and journalist.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Jim Fouratt Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Papers relating to political and counterculture activism, 1965-1974. II. Papers relating to gay rights and AIDS activism, 1968-2016. III. Papers relating to nightclub and band management, circa 1978-2000s. IV. Band demos and recordings, circa 1980s. V. Video recordings of band performances, 1979-1987. VI. Buttons, 1965-1980s. VII. T-shirts, 1977-1990. VIII. Realia, circa 1980s-2000s.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.
Boxes 55-142, 208 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 209 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Collection Description note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

did.unittitle: Papers relating to political and counterculture activism

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains papers documenting Jim Fouratt's involvement with political and counterculture movements and events of the late 1960s and early 1970s, including the Youth International Party (Yippies), the Purple Star Tribe, the 1967 Central Park Be In, and the 1969 Woodstock Music and Art Fair. Papers include correspondence, photographs, artwork, printed material, ephemera, notebooks, and other papers. Also includes scripts and photographs documenting Fouratt's work as an actor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of the results of Jim Fouratt's multiple requests to varying United States governement agencies for information about files kept about him under his legal name and his aliases.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs, calendar page, flyer, notes, and a poem from Fouratt's trip to Gay Pride Week in Austin Texas with the Purple Star Tribe and the Stompin' Lilac Society. Some of the photographs were used for the groups' work on Iconoclast Comes Out Volume 1, number 31.

did.unittitle: Press releases, flyers, and political tracts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes papers from the Communications Company, White Panther Party, Yippie!, and other groups. Also includes drawings, correspondence, and other related papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Digger flyers from 1967 for a "Free Store," the "Be In" at Central Park and others from the "Communication Company/NY."

did.unittitle: Freelandia airline membership card and informational materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Much of the the correspondence is addressed to "Digger" Jim.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 114 handbills created by Peter Max for the Easter Central Park Be In on March 26th, 1967. Also includes the original mechanical.

did.unittitle: Be In flyers, notes and newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of protests, actors, musicians, activists, and Fouratt as an actor in a play.

did.unittitle: Photographs of protests, actors, musicians, and activists
Woodstock Music and Art Fair passes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a press parking pass, a performer pass for Chip Monck from Woodstock Ventures, and a courtesy pass for photographer Peter Hujar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes about Richard Neville with a photograph of Fouratt at a protest, poetry and other materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a note and magazine clipping from Clive Davis.

did.unittitle: 3 reel to reel tapes by Al Cooper, It's a Beautiful Day, and one titled "Rocks Greatest Hits"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Advertising materials, including photographs and clippings, for the band It's a Beautiful Day
Script for Bent by Martin Sherman, newspaper clipping, and ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the following titles: The Motion of History by Amiri Baraka, The Freaking Out of Stephanie Blake by Richard Chandler, Isolation by Lee Kissman, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a "Purple Star Tribe house copy" of Quicksilver Times , July 1971, and an annotated copy of Iconoclast Comes Out volume 1 number 3, from Dallas News. Also includes a Weather Underground poster inscribed "To Digger - with love Camo, Lorenzo, Possom."

did.unittitle: Papers relating to gay rights and AIDS activism

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains papers documenting Jim Fouratt's activism in support of gay rights and in reaction to the AIDS epidemic. Documentation includes correspondence, writings, printed materials, notebooks, posters, flyers, and other papers. A significant portionof the papers relate to Fouratt's involvement with groups including the Gay Liberation Front, Wipe Out AIDS, the Federation of AIDS Related Organizations, and ACT UP.

did.unittitle: New York AIDS Network, The Center Proposal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of materials relating to planning a center for the gay and lesbian community.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook contains Jim Fouratt's notes on AIDS and substance abuse, gay and lesbian parenting, and Europe's responce to the AIDS crisis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials documenting Jim Fouratt's involvment with ACT UP, Gay Activist Alliance of New York, and the 1988 NY War Conference. Also includes correspondence, anti-AMA propaganda, and the Last Will and Testament of Max Navarre (aka Michael Hurlebaus).

did.unittitle: Printed and photocopied materials relating to mental health from the AIDS Institute of New York and other organizations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes, flyers, publications, and correspondence from Wipe Out AIDS (WOA) and the Federation of AIDS Related Organizations (FARO). Publications include How to Have Sex In An Epidemic , The Case for the Temporary Closure of Commercial Sex Establishments During the AIDS crisis: Throwing Out the Baths with the Bath Water , and others.

did.unittitle: How to Have Sex In An Epidemic and related materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the original pamphlet How to Have Sex In An Epidemic , related materials, and other printed materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, printed materials, a ledger, and a funding proposal to New York State AIDS institute. Also includes publications about diet and nutritional supplements.

did.unittitle: Gay Liberation Front and radical feminist materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents, flyers, and printed material relating to the Gay Liberation front and radical feminism. Publications include A Gay Manifesto by Carle Wittman, The GLF calendar, pamphlets from The Feminists, Gay Flames pamphlets, Echo of Sappho, early writings by T. Grace Atkinson, Diana di Prima's Revolutionary Letters published by The Communication Company/NY, and others.

did.unittitle: Gay rights and AIDS health information

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, photocopies, and newspaper clippings about AIDS public health issues. Also includes materials relating to the advocacy group ACT UP.

did.unittitle: Wipe Out AIDS and AIDS health information

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flyers, correspondence, and related materials for Wipe Out AIDS. Also includes information on AIDS health and diet, notes, and printed materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Political tracts include "Lesbians and the Health Care System," "20 Questions about Homosexuality," "Notes Toward a Gay Consiousness," and others. Also includes related correspondence and ephemera.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Political tracts, correspondence, and other materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence largely pertaining Fouratt's arrest in 1971. Other materials include a Gay Liberation Front political tract, typescripts, flyers, and ephemera. Also includes a copy of Jean Genet's "The Man Condemned to Death" translated by Dinana di Prima and a lightly annotated carbon typscript of John Giorno's "Cum."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Symbionese Liberation Army poster, printed guide to May Day in Washington D.C., and a Purple Star Tribe house copy of "Iconoclast Comes Out".

did.unittitle: Weather Underground rainbow painting and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Jim Fouratt, Michael Bumblbee, and Gay Liberation Front protests.

did.unittitle: Notes, faxes, correspondence, and publications relating to ACT UP
Gay and lesbian community reference materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Lesbian and Gay Directory of Services and Resources, National Gay and Lesbian Task Force press materials, and Twelve Steps and Twelve Traditions of Recovery for Gay Men's Incest and Sexual Abuse Survivors Anonymous.

did.unittitle: Faxes, correspondence, flyers, notes, and ephemera concerning sexual politics and AIDS health

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of meeting minutes, including a proposal for a documentary film about FARO.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Jim Fouratt and Michael Callen on how bath club owners could raise conciousness of AIDS with their patrons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by others, clippings, cartoons, and other papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies of the gay community publication and related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes papers titled "The Norlin paper" and "Letter from Michio to Wipe Out AIDS Friends" and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials relating to gay rights and AIDS in Hait and Japan.

did.unittitle: ACT UP materials and gay rights ephemera
News clippings and ephemera related to safe sex and gay rights
Gay rights and AIDS awareness art projects
Miscellaneous gay rights and AIDS activism materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials from various regional organiziations in the northeast United States.

did.unittitle: Lesbian and Gay Service Center founding documents
Transcript of an interview covering Fouratt's life from birth through the early 1970s

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes one partial, untitled typescript and one manuscript titled "Comming Out Of The WPP."

did.unittitle: Newspaper and magazine clippings and ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes records relating to Baltimore H.E.R.O., New York AIDS Network, National Gay Health Education Foundation, and other groups. Also includes conference papers and reports.

did.unittitle: Healthy eating and wok cooking cartoon
Papers relating to nightclub and band management

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of papers related to Jim Fouratt's work as a manager of nightclubs and musical acts. Papers include correspondence, writings, legal documents, photographs, press releases, mailing lists, address books, business records, promotional ephemera, clippings, and other materials. Nightclub management materials document Fouratt's work with Average Normal, Inc., Dare Entertainment, Inc., Danceteria, Hurrah!, Studio 54, CBGB's, Limelight, and others. Documentation of musical acts includes papers relating to Richard Lloyd, Swans, Two Nice Girls, Pylon, Ornette Coleman, Hot Lunch, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Managment notes and film criticism.

did.unittitle: Legal and incorporation files for Dare Entertainment Inc.
Dare Entertainment Inc. promotion related correspondence
Legal contracts with The Radio Band of America and related correspondence
Promotional materials for bands and clubs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inlcudes flyers, cards, notepads, and other ephemera.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes music-related ephemera and correspondence, invoices and other materials pertaining to Richard Lloyd, Tim Miller, Animal Slaves, and others.

did.unittitle: Danceteria flyers, promotional materials, and newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials from Blitz, On The Waterfront, and Modern Classix.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes opperational memos, correspondence, artist contracts, legal papers, and other business papers.

did.unittitle: Ideas and clippings for promotional materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes magazine and newspaper clippings, photographs, and other materials used to create advertising and flyers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of locations around Danceteria and other clubs, autographs, and pictures of Jim Fouratt with various bands.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of photographs and other materials including the following:
Cooper, Dennis. "XMAS 1982" Folio w/ photographs inscribed by Cooper to Fouratt and Miller.
Tim and Dona McAdams. "Democracy in America" inscribed by Miller to Fouratt. Signed by Miller and McAdams. #31 in an edition of 50. 1984.

did.unittitle: Post-Punk and New Wave band files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of correspondence, promotional materials, show flyers, and photographs relating to Fad Gadget [Frank Tovey], Young Marble Giants, R.E.M., Jayne County, Human Sexual Response, The Grown Ups, and others.

did.unittitle: Correspondence and other business papers related to Rhino Records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes IOU documentation from Keith Lavine of Public Image Ltd. for money borrowed from Jim Fouratt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional materials, band flyers and photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional materials, band flyers, photographs, related reciepts and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accounting, consulting, publicity, and talent booking records for Studio 54. Also includes VIP mailing lists, letters of support from Fouratt's tenure at Danceteria.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Postcards, stickers, and other materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials from Hurrah!, Danceteria, and Average Normal, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes guest lists.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes posters, stickers and postcards.

did.unittitle: Operational and business papers relating to club and talent management

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Postcards, flyers, finacial records, and statements. Also includes concert ephemera, photographs, and Danceteria pinback buttons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional materials relate to Limelight, Max's Kansas City, and Danceteria, as well as musical acts that Fouratt managed. Business papers include correspondence, reciepts, insurance, taxes, and contracts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains lyrics, photographs, and artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes acting resume, therapy notebook, and a pocket edition Mao Tse-Tung.

did.unittitle: Musical act management, Two Nice Girls

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of materials documenting Fouratt's management of the band Two Nice Girls, including promotional materials, maquettes for album artwork, contracts, press clippings, reciepts, and other related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of papers documenting Fouratt's management of former Television guitarist Richard Lloyd, includes promotional materials, contracts, press clippings, reciepts, and other related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include, correspondence, receipts, legal papers, ephemera, and newspaper clippings.

did.unittitle: Receipts, travel documentation, and other business related documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and photocopies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes artwork by Michael Shore, photographs, flyers, and other materials for the band Hot Lunch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a Throbbing Gristle press release,and a "Trials of Sylvia Likens" poster signed by Kate Millett, 1978, and others papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The bulk of these materials relate to the legalities of rent, and eviction.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes minutes, notes, lists, reports, and other related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes statements, canceled checks, reciepts, and other related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence from friends, artists, and musicians related to Fouratt's club and band mamangment.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: 33 rpm record by The Scientific Americans

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes essays, news clippings, and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: DARE management files related to managing Richard Lloyd including reciepts, legal files, promotional materials, correspondence, newspaper clippings, photographs, and other related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: DARE management files related to managing Pylon including legal files, promotional materials, correspondence, newspaper clippings, photographs, and other related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: DARE management files related to managing Ornette Coleman including reciepts, legal files, promotional materials, correspondence, newspaper clippings, sheet music, and other related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs and sheet music in an envelope titled "photos of what alcohol did to me".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of bands and musicians including LowLife, Purnac, Tetes Noires (with correspondence), Love Tractor, Minutemen, Last Roundup, My Sin, REDD KROSS, Tex and the Horseheads, Carmaig de Forest, Marlene, Angela Bowie (with correspondence), The Hood, Bond Bergland, Xotika, Trigger and Her Thrill Kings, The Wygals, Meat Puppets, David Thomas, Nora York, and others.

did.unittitle: Band and concert flyers, DIY press kits, and other ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes, lists, legal papers, and other business materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monologue based on King Lear.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Invitations for Danceteria and Modern Classix events, announcements for concerts, postcards, and other promotional materials.

did.unittitle: Danceteria and Modern Classix advertising maquettes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unsorted photographs and negatives of bands, parties, clubs, art exhibits, and the Fouratt family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from Danceteria and artist promotional shots, as well as a number of original photographs including portraits of Patti Smith, Lynn Goldsmith, Caberet Voltaire, Stiff Little Fingers, and the Go-Go's. Also includes newspaper clippings, notes, correspondence, and ephemera.

did.unittitle: 45 rpm records by Chi-Pig, Plastics, and Delta

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Invitations, promotional cards, and passes for events

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials for events presented by Fouratt at, Studio 54, Limelight, and Danceteria for shows by Richard Lloyd, Romeo Void, Glenn Branca, and others. Also included are postcards sent to Fouratt by friends.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included are band files with promotional photos, and press releases, notes, and related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebooks, mailing lists, and other related materials.

did.unittitle: Tuxedomoon promotional materials and binder "Minette-recollections of a part-time lady"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs are from a trip Fouratt took to Moscow, Russia that coincided with the Russian Federation of Artists inviting 100 American painters to exhibit there.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional packs for independent films, many from the 1995 Sundance Film Festival.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, newspaper and magazine clippings, flyers, photographs, notes, and other related materials.

did.unittitle: Clippings of Fouratt's music and lifestyle column "Far Truths" in Hits magazine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Postcards, paper dolls, band flyers, photographs, and other materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, photographs, and legal documents.

did.unittitle: Music and art magazines and photocopies
Event flyers and Pylon promotional pack

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files include financial statements, technical specifications for the club, security, booking, promotion, and event information.

did.unittitle: Band tour schedules and other materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes posters, flyers, slides, and photographs. Subjects includes Bob Dylan, Mick Jagger, Pacific Gas and Electric, Santana, Lotti Golden, and others.

did.unittitle: "On The Waterfront" events and other business papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes materials from Danceteria and Average Normal.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Neil Young, Aphex Twin, The Stone Roses, The Verve, Brian Wilson, Jesus and Mary Chain, Ministry, Peru Ubu, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes guest lists, band contracts, press releases, correspondence, and other materials from Danceteria, Studio 54, and Average Normal Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of the bands X, The B-52's, Bauhaus, The Feelies, Two Nice Girls, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, invitations, flyers, maquettes, and band files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes includes correspondence and other papers from bands and manangers seeking to book shows in New York as well as letters from Joe Brainard to Jim Fouratt.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes files related to contracts, royalties, promotion, budget, and tour itinerary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mailing lists for Average Normal, Inc. and related files including materials relating to operations at Studio 54 and Danceteria. Also includes photographs, negatives, and ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes legal papers, receipts, correspondence, and other papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes promotional materials, reviews, and flyers from Adam Ant, Radiohead, Clannad, Alec Finn, Ani DiFranco, PJ Harvey, Better Than Ezra, Prodigy, and others.

did.unittitle: Papers relating to DARE Management and On The Waterfont
Drafts of articles written for The Village Voice
Photocopies and clippings of articles written by Fouratt for Ear Candy
Transcript of an interview with Fouratt about working for Columbia records and in the music industry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newspaper and magazine clippings, flyers, and photocopies of promotional advertisments.

did.unittitle: 2 reel to reel tapes by Polyrock and Lou Miami

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of demos and other recordings of musical acts active in the early- to mid-1980s. Most were sent to Jim Fouratt by artists seeking to perform at clubs like Hurrah!, Danceteria, The Underground, Pepermint Lounge, and others. The recordings document the transition of the New York scene from Punk to Post-Punk and New Wave, and represent a diverse range of hundreds of artists including notable acts such as Miracle Legion, They Might Be Giants, Human Sexual Response, Bow Wow Wow, Living Colour, Pylon, The Replacements, R.E.M., Ornette Coleman, Daniel Johnston, New Order, Ministry, Richard Lloyd, Suzanne Vega, Yo La Tengo, The Misfits, Hüsker Dü, Bad Brains, The B-52's, Two Nice Girls, and many others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged roughly in order as received.

did.unittitle: Carmaig de Forest + Band - Demo of Love
Los Microwaves - Silent Screamers, I Can't Say
Francois Kevorkian - Music for the Mind: The Dance
Richard Thorne and the Side Effects
Wake Up and Cry, Angel Corpus-Christi
A Move To The Right, Best of Eden, In Dark Towers, Wrinklemuzik
Pinhead - You Don't Like Me, Do Ya?
Face of the Future - Manhattan Project
Face of the Future - Manhattan Project
A Few - It's A Wonderful Life
The Delusions, Drink And Be Mary
The Mindless Members featuring Joe Hampton
Lucky Del Ray and the Raydels
4 Skins w/ the Chiclets, Sax Fifth Ave
John Eddie and the Front Street Runners
Tirez Tirez - Story Of The Year
Tirez Tirez - Story Of The Year
Tirez Tirez - Story Of The Year
Mikel Rouse Broken Consort - A Walk in the Woods
Poets: Will Bennet, George Therese Dickenson
Michael Callen - Songs for the AIDies
Giant Ears Listen In At Dawn, Radio Moscow
Eye and I (Melvin Gibbs and D.K. Dyson)
Fade to Black - Corridors of Gender
Mikel Rouse (Tirez Tirez) - Story of the Year
Linda Hudes Power Trio - Century XXI, Antigua
Hüsker Dü - We Wish You A Merry Christmas
Method Actors - Last Day of the Decade
The Shades Crash Course In Science
Das Damen - Too Hot For Hollywood
Great Plains - Born in a Barn
Ramsey Najm and The Nervous System
Two Nice Girls - All songs & arrangements
Love Don't Need a Reason, The Healing Power of Love
The President of the United States
Max Navarre, Demo (The Max Navarre Band)
Michael Pablo Dugan, Tape for Danceteria

did.physdesc: 131 video recordings on varying video cassette formats.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged roughly in order as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of video recordings of live band performances, mostly recorded at the New York nightclub Danceteria. Performers include Richard Lloyd, Mission of Burma, Liquid Liquid, Sonic Youth, Pylon, ESG, and others. Also includes some video recordings related to the AIDS epidemic.

did.unittitle: Richard Lloyd Live at The Ritz
The Fleshtones in The Underground Fouratt
Richard Lloyd Live at The Ritz
The Fall Perverted by Language Bis
The Right Side of My Brain

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of buttons collected by Jim Fouratt. Button subjects span a variety of political causes and many musical acts active during the 1980s.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Buttons are roughly organized into groups of political and music-related buttons.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Up to two copies of each button are pinned to foam core in boxes 143-151. Subsequent duplicate copies of pins are stored in boxes 152-154. All pins in boxes 152-154 are represented in boxes 143-151.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 24 total pins, included are of The Black Panthers, the Youth International Party, Vientnam protests, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes bands such as: Mo-Dettes, Buzzcocks, Viletones, Sex Pistols, The Records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes bands such as: The Knack, Suburban Lawns, Bunnydrums, Screamers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes bands such as: 2 Nice Girls, The Records, The Boys, SVT, The Revillos, Joy Division, Monomania

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes bands such as: Angela Bowie, Chrome, Ruts, Tuxedo Moon, The Romantics, Peter Dayton, Moot

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes bands such as: Hot Lunch, The Mekons, Floundering, The Privates, Catholic Girls. Extra space for more pins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes causes such as: AIDS, Safe Sex, Lesbian and Gay Rights, Marijuana, Keith Harring

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes causes such as: City Council, War, Nicaragua, Senate, Actors, Musicians and Prisoners

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes causes such as: Condoms, IRA, Military, City Council, Presidents. Extra space for more pins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of t-shirts collected and worn by Jim Fouratt. T-shirt subjects include musical acts ranging from David Bowie and Iggy Pop to Gang of Four and Kraftwork, the Danceteria nightclub, and gay rights and AIDS-related activism.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged roughly in order as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tank top.

did.unittitle: Quote from Salman Rushdie "The Satanic Verses"
The Pleasure Head - A Turn of the Screw
Live Like Her - Nguyen Thi Binh
Dance to the BLITZ - Rock New Wave

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The back of the shirt lists the bands Pigbag, Pulsallama, Shriekback, and Maximum Joy.

did.unittitle: Blue t-shirt with a pink triangle in a pink circle
CDC CD Atlanta 1990 - Lick the sodomy laws!
John Cale - Honi Soit Qui Mal Y Pense

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shirt commemorates the "White Night Riots".

did.unittitle: The Pop Group - We are all prostitutes
Danceteria - We serve the best music. Live-Recorded-Hot!
Danceteria - We serve the best music. Live-Recorded-Hot!
Homemade t-shirt for the band Hot Lunch

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Realia consists of concert passes, stickers, a Keith Haring puzzle, a stamp, drink tokens, and other objects collected by Jim Fouratt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Studio 54 stickers, a Keith Haring puzzle, x-rays, penguin stamp used in Average Normal materials, and other objects.

did.unittitle: Broken buttons, drink tokens, costume jewelry, and other materials
